title: Skill Comprehension Eval
description: Binding shape for skills/<name>/evals/comprehension.json — the gradeable, dimension-tagged comprehension eval consumed by gate 8 (the comprehension grader) and the false-canonicality verifier at skill-graph/scripts/check-audit-manifest.js. v1 authored 2026-05-25 in response to the 2026-05-25 multi-model review surfacing this as the highest-priority canonicalization gap (Opus G2#3 CRITICAL; GPT-5.5 G3#1 PARTIAL). Provenance: ADR-0011 (Audit Status verdicts) defines comprehension_verdict; this schema defines the artifact the grader evaluates. The rubric dimensions are analogy / application / boundary / definition / mental_model / purpose / taxonomy, with misconception reserved for common wrong-reading cases.

skill_name.description: Kebab-case name; MUST match the parent directory name (e.g., 'methodical' for skills/methodical/evals/comprehension.json) and the `name:` field in the corresponding SKILL.md.

subject.description: Human-readable subject of the skill (e.g., 'Methodical Execution', 'Bayesian Reasoning'). Used by the grader prompt as the concept under evaluation.

adjacent_concepts.description: Related skill names or concepts useful to the grader for boundary checks. Optional but recommended; e.g., ['intellectual honesty', 'quality-doctrine', 'self-review-pattern'].

evals.description: Per-dimension comprehension cases. Minimum 5 (the skill-audit-loop/SKILL_AUDIT_LOOP.md § Part 3 — Per-Skill Audit Runbook § 4c floor). Practitioners typically author 7 covering all rubric dimensions. Below 5 fails the gate-8 floor; below 7 records a dimension-coverage warning.

id.description: Stable integer identifier, unique within this file. The grader reports per-case results by id.

dimension.description: Which comprehension rubric dimension this case targets. The 7 dimensions in active use as of 2026-05-25 are: definition, mental_model, purpose, boundary, taxonomy, analogy, application. The 8th value `misconception` mirrors the Understanding-field name from skill-metadata-protocol/SKILL_METADATA_PROTOCOL.md § Understanding — currently unused in the corpus but reserved for future cases. Each rubric dimension SHOULD appear at least once across a 7-case set.

prompt.description: The scenario / question the agent is asked to answer. Must require the skill's specific judgment (not pattern-matching or trivia). Negative expectations (what the answer must NOT say) are encoded in the grader rubric, not here.

substance.description: What kind of knowledge the case probes. `concept` for definitional/principle cases; `procedure` for sequencing/workflow cases; `boundary` for owner/scope cases. Default for new cases: `concept`.

calibration.description: How the grader scores the answer. `semantic` (default) — paraphrase OK if the meaning is right; `exact-match` — specific tokens / numbers required; `structural` — answer must match a specified form (list, table, predicate).

truth_mode.description: What grounds the grader's correctness check. `conceptual_correctness` — universal principle, no repo grounding; `conceptual_correctness_plus_repo_application` (most common today) — universal principle PLUS check that the answer applies it to this repo; `spec_grounded` — answer must match a named external spec / standard; `principle_grounded` — answer must derive from a named first principle.

skill_type.description: Grader hint for the case's knowledge shape: concept, procedure, router, or overlay. This is eval-case metadata, not a mapping to the retired SKILL.md `type` axis.

criticality.description: Per-case weight. `critical` cases are gate-blocking failures (a missed `critical` case alone can demote the comprehension_verdict to SHALLOW). `low` cases inform the grader but do not block. Default: `medium`.

negative_expectation.description: Optional explicit statement of what the answer must NOT say or do. Catches silent scope reduction and softened-failure responses per skill-audit-loop/SKILL_AUDIT_LOOP.md § Part 3 — Per-Skill Audit Runbook § 4c. Recommended for `boundary` and `critical` cases.
